description: Access to patient or clinical data should be auditable.
body: Record actor, purpose, time, and data scope for sensitive access where system policy requires. Use when healthcare engineering work touches PHI flows, patient identifiers, consent state, audit events, clinical workflow boundaries, and access logs. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include PHI minimization checks, access-control tests, audit-log assertions, consent-path review, and clinical owner approval where required. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves medical advice automation, PHI disclosure, unauthorized access, missing audit records, and regulated workflow ambiguity, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
